title: garlicky, flaky, indulgent egyptian lamb tagine
description: lamb and spices slow cooked in a flavorful aromatic stew, inspired by egyptian cuisine. served over a bed of fluffy basmati rice.
instructions: heat 2 tablespoons of ghee or vegetable oil in a large clay tajine over medium-high heat.
add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
remove the lamb from the pan and set aside.
add 2 cloves of minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
add 1 teaspoon of ground cumin, 1 teaspoon of ground coriander, and 1/2 teaspoon of ground cayenne pepper and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the lamb back to the pan, along with 1 can of diced tomatoes and 2 cups of chicken broth.
bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
stir in 2 tablespoons of all-purpose flour to thicken the stew.
season with salt and pepper to taste.
serve the lamb and sauce over a bed of fluffy basmati rice.
